events.title: Drone strike causes fire at Russian oil terminal
events.description: A drone strike ignited a fire at a major oil export terminal in Novorossiysk, Russia, during an overnight attack. The incident caused at least one injury and disrupted operations at the key Black Sea port.

events.title: Argentina reduces wheat, barley export duties
events.description: Argentina will reduce export duties for wheat and barley to 5.5% from 7.5% starting in June, aiming to ease pressure on producers ahead of the 2026-27 season. Soybean duties will also decrease from 7.5% to 5.5% in January 2027.

events.title: US-Iran deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US and Iran are nearing a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping and allow Iran to resume unrestricted oil exports as part of a 60-day ceasefire extension.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening Deal Near
events.description: The U.S. and Iran have outlined an agreement to extend their ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The deal has not yet been finalized.

events.title: Cocaine seizure on US-bound tanker disrupts freight
events.description: US authorities seized 227 kg of cocaine valued at $6.4m from a crude oil tanker arriving in Southern California. A Filipino seafarer has been charged in connection with the alleged cartel-linked smuggling operation.

events.title: Asia-Europe freight rates surge, WCI rises 6%
events.description: The Drewry World Container Index increased 6% to $2,712 per 40ft container, driven by a 15% rise in Asia-Europe spot rates, particularly from Shanghai to Rotterdam, signaling early peak season demand.

events.title: Chinese Coast Guard Ship Departs Pratas Islands Waters
events.description: A Chinese coast guard ship left waters near Taiwan's Pratas Islands after a tense standoff, raising concerns over regional tensions that could impact maritime freight routes.

events.title: LNG Tanker Exits Hormuz for India
events.description: An LNG tanker has exited the Strait of Hormuz heading to India for the first time since the war began, signaling a potential easing or shift in regional shipping disruptions.

events.title: Oman's strategic role in Hormuz dynamic
events.description: Oman is playing a pivotal role in the evolving Hormuz dynamic, influencing the potential permanence of the PGSA toll regime and affecting global maritime freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Sheskharis oil terminal
events.description: The Ukrainian military reported striking Russia's Sheskharis oil terminal and nearby Grushova oil depot on the Black Sea, potentially disrupting oil infrastructure and regional supply chain operations.

events.title: SCOTUS ruling impacts truckload brokerage sector
events.description: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled that truckload brokers can be held liable under state law for negligent hiring, leading to increased spot rates and potential long-term changes in the $800B+ truckload brokerage industry.

events.title: US Blockade Redirects 100 Vessels Near Iran
events.description: US forces have redirected 100 commercial vessels near Iranian ports as part of a six-week blockade, disrupting regional maritime traffic.

events.title: Gainesville Inland Port opens with daily rail service to Savannah
events.description: The Georgia Ports Authority's Gainesville Inland Port officially launched daily rail service to the Port of Savannah, enhancing freight connectivity for regional manufacturers.

events.title: Dali owners settle USD 2.25B Maryland claim
events.description: Grace Ocean and Synergy Marine have agreed to settle a USD 2.25 billion claim by Maryland related to the Dali's collision with the Francis Scott Key Bridge in March 2024.

events.title: MSC launches Ochna service for China-Vietnam trade
events.description: MSC has launched the Ochna service, providing direct and fast connectivity between key ports in China and Vietnam. The inaugural voyage, operated by the MSC Hailey Ann II, is scheduled to arrive in Dalian on June 19, 2026.

events.title: Iran conflict impacts Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The ongoing Iran conflict, now in its 12th week, continues to create uncertainty and potential disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Maersk blanks Northern Star sailing
events.description: Maersk has announced a seasonal blanking on its Northern Star service, affecting the Capitaine Baret on voyages 628S and 631N due to cargo seasonality and demand fluctuations on the Asia-Oceania corridor.

events.title: U.S.-China Agree on Hormuz Strait Toll Policy
events.description: During U.S. President Trump's visit to China, both nations agreed that tolls should not be imposed on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz. The agreement highlights potential policy influence on a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure risk
events.description: A prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz poses the greatest threat to global energy markets in decades, with over 11 million barrels per day of Gulf crude and condensate production currently curtailed, according to a Wood Mackenzie report.

events.title: MSC Elsa 3 Crew Detained in India
events.description: Seven crewmembers from the MSC Elsa 3 containership, which capsized and sank off the Indian coast a year ago, are still seeking release.

events.title: Asia-Europe container freight rates surge
events.description: Container spot rates on Asia-Europe routes have seen double-digit increases, with the Shanghai-Rotterdam leg rising 15% week-on-week to $2,773 per 40ft container, driven by sustained demand and carrier-imposed rate adjustments.

events.title: US transfers crew of seized Iranian ship to Pakistan
events.description: The US has transferred the crew of the seized Iranian container ship Touska to Pakistan as a confidence-building measure. The crew will be returned to Iran, indicating ongoing tensions affecting maritime operations.

events.title: Tanker fires warning shots near Somalia
events.description: A tanker in the Gulf of Aden reported that its security team fired warning shots on May 22 after a suspicious approach. The incident highlights ongoing security risks in the region affecting maritime freight.

events.title: Iran claims increased control over Hormuz Strait transits
events.description: Iran asserts that transits through the Strait of Hormuz are increasing under its control, citing improved coordination. These claims remain unsubstantiated and raise concerns about regional stability and control over this critical global shipping route.

events.title: Iceland port labour agreement averts strike
events.description: An agreement between Eimskip and the Seafarers’ Union of Iceland averts a planned strike at Sundahöfn port, which was scheduled to begin on May 26, 2026.

events.title: Wan Hai Lines implements rate restoration on Asia trades
events.description: Wan Hai Lines will apply a rate restoration (RR) on applicable Asia trades starting June 1, 2026, citing rising operating costs due to developments in the Middle East. The new rates include USD 100 per 20-foot container and USD 200 per 40-foot container.

events.title: Iran asserts control over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has published a new map asserting expanded control over the Strait of Hormuz, heightening tensions and prolonging the crisis for over 20,000 seafarers stranded on ships in the Gulf.

events.title: Hormuz Crisis Disrupts Tanker Trade
events.description: The ongoing Hormuz crisis is significantly impacting the global tanker trade, causing rerouting and increased freight rates. Frontline plc reported its strongest quarterly earnings in over two decades due to these disruptions.

events.title: Port Houston cargo dip in April
events.description: Port Houston experienced its first container slowdown since early 2025 in April, though officials anticipate a recovery in May with improved cargo volumes.

events.title: Singamas CEO indicted in container price-fixing probe
events.description: Singamas Container Holdings CEO Teo Siong Seng has been indicted by the US Department of Justice for conspiring to fix container prices. The case involves collusion with other manufacturers and could impact global container supply and pricing dynamics.

events.title: Caofeidian Port handles first full-load ultra-large ore carrier
events.description: The 400,000-metric-ton-class ore carrier Yuan Jian Hai berthed fully loaded at Caofeidian Port, marking the first regular full-load berthing of an ultra-large ore vessel at the port. The vessel carried Brazilian iron ore and used the Laotieshan channel.

events.title: Morocco expands ports for global logistics hub
events.description: Morocco is accelerating a major port expansion strategy, investing billions to develop maritime infrastructure and logistics corridors, aiming to position itself as a key trade gateway between Europe, Africa, and the Atlantic.

events.title: Middle East conflict impacts ocean freight rates
events.description: The Middle East conflict has caused war risk premiums and bunker fuel surcharges to surge, with spot rates on directly-affected trades spiking to around $4,000 per container.

events.title: Iran proposes permanent toll for Hormuz Strait shipping
events.description: Iran is discussing with Oman to establish a permanent toll system for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, requiring payments for navigation and security. The proposal could impact global shipping flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure risks food price crisis
events.description: The UN Food and Agriculture Organization warns that a closure of the Strait of Hormuz could cause a severe global food price crisis within six to 12 months. The strait handles about 30% of global urea exports and is a key route for energy and fertilizer trade.

events.title: Iran activates new export node east of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has begun using the Kuh Mubarak offshore loading buoy to export Iranian Heavy crude, primarily to China, marking a new export node east of the Hormuz Strait amid U.S. enforcement and a live toll regime.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Transits Face Heightened Risk
events.description: A new industry report highlights increased risks for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, including missile and drone attacks, as well as erratic traffic patterns.

events.title: COSCO launches Gdynia feeder service with Diamond Line
events.description: COSCO SHIPPING is launching a new Gdynia feeder service in collaboration with Diamond Line, starting in early June. The service will offer two weekly calls from Gdynia to major European hub ports, enhancing network connectivity and operations.

events.title: USMCA renewal uncertainty impacts trade flows
events.description: Fading hopes for a timely renewal of the USMCA trade pact before 1 July are creating uncertainty, affecting trade and investment decisions across the US, Canada, and Mexico.

events.title: Iran Proposes Control Over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran proposed managing the Strait of Hormuz through an insurance-based framework, requiring all vessels to file cargo declarations with a new Iranian maritime authority before transit. This move could significantly impact global oil shipping and freight flows.

events.title: Russia's shadow fleet used for hybrid warfare
events.description: A new investigation reveals that Russia's shadow fleet of oil tankers is being used as a hybrid warfare platform, enabling undersea sabotage and drone surveillance over military and critical infrastructure in the Baltic and North Sea regions.

events.title: Merchant ship trapped in Gulf war zone
events.description: A merchant ship is caught in the conflict in the Gulf, with the Strait of Hormuz turning into a battlefield. Captain Mohit Kohli describes the dangers faced by commercial shipping, including missiles, drones, and misinformation, highlighting the lack of preparedness for such a scenario.

events.title: Panama Canal near capacity due to Hormuz disruptions
events.description: Increased US energy exports to Asia, driven by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, have pushed Panama Canal traffic to near maximum capacity, with daily transits up 8% year-on-year in 2026.

events.title: First oil tanker exits Hormuz since Iran war began
events.description: Japan is set to receive the first oil tanker to exit the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began, signaling a potential easing of disruptions in a key global energy chokepoint.

events.title: MSC updates emergency fuel surcharge for Red Sea and East Africa routes
events.description: MSC has updated its emergency fuel surcharge for cargo moving between Northern Europe, the Red Sea, and East Africa, effective June 1–15, 2026, due to ongoing disruptions in global marine operations.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new PSS charges on key routes
events.description: CMA CGM announced a new Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) of $500 per TEU effective 1 June 2026 for cargo from Asia to North Europe, impacting trade flows and freight costs.

events.title: Rhenus opens Jordan land corridor for Europe-Middle East freight
events.description: Rhenus has launched an alternative overland transport corridor through Jordan to maintain cargo flows between Europe and the Middle East, bypassing traditional routes affected by regional disruptions. The new route connects Europe and Türkiye with Gulf markets including Saudi Arabia, UAE, and others.

events.title: Container Spot Rates Rise in Early Peak Season
events.description: Container freight rates are increasing as carriers raise prices on major East-West trade lanes, signaling the start of the traditional peak shipping season. This trend reflects strong demand and ongoing supply chain adjustments.

events.title: Iran claims control of waters south of UAE port
events.description: Iran's new Hormuz authority claims control of waters south of UAE's Fujairah port, which is used to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. This development could impact global shipping through this vital conduit.

events.title: CMA CGM Launches Mekong Transpacific Express Service
events.description: CMA CGM is launching the Mekong Transpacific Express to provide direct and reliable shipping connections from Vietnam to the U.S. West Coast, enhancing Transpacific trade routes.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption boosts Panama Canal transits
events.description: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz have led to a 8% year-over-year increase in Panama Canal transits, particularly driven by the tanker sector as US energy exports to the Pacific rise.

events.title: Georgia Ports Volume Decline Due to Costs and Market Softness
events.description: The Georgia Ports Authority reported a decline in container volumes at the Port of Savannah, citing higher operating costs and a weaker market as contributing factors.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz LNG supply disruption
events.description: The International Gas Union warns that the ongoing disruption of LNG supply through the Strait of Hormuz, due to curtailed Qatari and UAE capacity, is critical for the global gas market.

events.title: Maersk Feeder Ship Grounded in Nigeria
events.description: A Maersk-operated feeder containership ran aground in Nigeria’s Bonny Channel after colliding with a barge, blocking port operations.

events.title: MSC integrates Port of Kribi into intermodal network
events.description: MSC has integrated the Port of Kribi in Cameroon into its intermodal logistics network, enhancing cargo connectivity between Cameroon and inland markets like Chad and the Central African Republic.

events.title: Truck crash kills two on CA Highway 99
events.description: A Freightliner Cascadia operated by Amritsar Trans Inc. rear-ended three vehicles on Highway 99 near Lodi, California, killing two young men on May 19, 2026. The driver fled the scene, and the carrier has a history of regulatory issues.

events.title: Boeing Delays Impact Cargo Fleet Strategies
events.description: Boeing's extended delivery delays are forcing cargo carriers to rethink long-term fleet strategies, with some opting for interim capacity solutions while remaining committed to Boeing.

events.title: Panama Canal near capacity due to Hormuz crisis
events.description: Increased U.S. energy exports to Asia and the Pacific are pushing the Panama Canal near capacity, driven by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Iran and Oman Discuss Hormuz Toll Regime
events.description: Iran is discussing with Oman the establishment of a permanent toll system to formalize control over maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new PSS charges on key trade routes
events.location: Global (China to Africa, Mediterranean, North America)
events.description: CMA CGM announced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) of up to $300 per TEU on several trade routes, effective 25 May 2026, impacting freight costs between China and West Africa, Mediterranean, and North America.

events.title: DoJ indicts Chinese container makers on price-fixing
events.description: The U.S. Justice Department has indicted four major Chinese shipping container manufacturers and seven executives for participating in a global price-fixing conspiracy, which could impact container pricing and availability in global supply chains.

events.title: Intra-Asia container rates hit 2-year high
events.description: Intra-Asia container freight rates have reached a two-year high, averaging $939 per 40ft container as demand increases ahead of the peak season. Rates on the Shanghai-Southeast Asia route also rose 31% year-on-year to $570 per TEU.

events.title: US indicts Chinese container firms for price fixing
events.description: The US Justice Department has indicted major Chinese container manufacturers, including CIMC and CXIC Group, for price fixing and restricting equipment. The case involves collusion among companies and executives, potentially impacting global container supply and pricing.

events.title: UK ETS implementation impacts shipping compliance
events.description: The UK Emissions Trading Scheme (UK ETS) is set to expand to the maritime sector in July, creating new compliance and cost challenges for shipping companies. Industry experts warn against short-term 'buy-to-comply' strategies due to potential long-term financial risks.

events.title: U.S. Marines board Iranian oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. Marines boarded an Iranian-flagged oil tanker, M/T Celestial Sea, in the Gulf of Oman after it was suspected of violating a U.S. blockade. The incident highlights ongoing tensions that could disrupt regional shipping and oil flows.

events.title: IMO Condemns Iran's Hormuz Attacks
events.description: The International Maritime Organization condemned Iran's attacks on merchant ships near the Strait of Hormuz and called for a safe-passage framework. The resolution was proposed by the UAE during the 111th Maritime Safety Committee session.

events.title: Mexican aid ship arrives in Cuba
events.description: A Chinese-owned cargo ship carrying humanitarian aid from Mexico and Uruguay arrived in Cuba. The shipment highlights ongoing international support amid political and economic tensions.

events.title: Capesize bulkers collide in Singapore Strait
events.description: Two Capesize bulkers collided in the Singapore Strait on Tuesday, with one vessel departing an anchorage and encountering the other. The incident is expected to cause local shipping delays and raise safety concerns in the busy waterway.

events.title: Iran establishes Persian Gulf Strait Authority
events.description: Iran has formally defined its regulatory jurisdiction over the Strait of Hormuz and established the Persian Gulf Strait Authority to manage transit through this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: CMA CGM raises Asia-Europe FAK rates in June 2026
events.description: CMA CGM announced increased Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for Asia to North Europe, Mediterranean, and North Africa routes from June 1 to 14, 2026. The rate changes reflect industry signals impacting freight costs during this period.

events.title: CMA CGM launches Mekong Transpacific Express service
events.description: CMA CGM has launched the Mekong Transpacific Express (MTE), a new weekly service offering direct container shipping between Vietnam and the US West Coast. The service aims to enhance connectivity and efficiency for trade between the two regions.

events.title: UAE-Oman multimodal corridor launched
events.description: A new multimodal trade corridor has been established between UAE's Sharjah Ports/Customs and Oman Customs, with Sohar Port as the primary gateway. The initiative aims to improve customs clearance, reduce freight costs, and enhance regional logistics connectivity.

events.title: Indonesia centralizes coal, palm oil, ferroalloy exports via state firm
events.description: Indonesia announced a policy to centralize all exports of coal, palm oil, and ferroalloys through a state-run trading enterprise, citing revenue losses due to under-invoicing and transfer pricing. The move has caused uncertainty in global commodity markets.

events.title: Coordinated VLCC transits resume through Hormuz Strait
events.description: Three VLCCs transited the Strait of Hormuz outbound in a coordinated manner, signaling a potential resumption of guided passage through the critical oil chokepoint after nearly 12 weeks of disruption.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Oil Flows Delayed Until 2027
events.description: Full oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz will not return before the first or second quarter of 2027, according to ADNOC, even if the Middle East conflict ends immediately.

events.title: UAE crude oil pipeline bypassing Hormuz reaches 50% completion
events.description: The UAE's new crude oil pipeline designed to bypass the Strait of Hormuz is 50% complete, according to ADNOC CEO Sultan Al Jaber. The development comes as Iran has largely closed the strait to non-Iranian vessels since February, causing energy price and inflation concerns.

events.title: ARA Bunker Fuel Loading Delays and Stock Decline
events.description: Bunker fuel suppliers in the ARA hub are experiencing loading delays at terminals, increasing lead times from 4-5 days to 10 days. Fuel oil stocks in the ARA have also dropped by 13% in May compared to April.

events.title: Asia-Europe freight cost volatility due to Middle East instability
events.description: Instability in key Middle East shipping corridors is causing unpredictable costs for Europe-bound freight. Rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope, fluctuating insurance, and changing fuel surcharges are making final costs uncertain during transit.

events.title: Iran Implements Transit Toll Regime at Hormuz Strait
events.description: Iran is formalizing a transit-toll regime at the Strait of Hormuz, with reports of $2 million per-transit fees paid in yuan and Bitcoin to IRGC-linked wallets. This could disrupt global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Ukrainian drones strike Russian Black Sea fleet and port
events.description: Ukrainian drone forces attacked a Russian patrol vessel, a cargo ship, and port infrastructure in the Black Sea, extending their reach into protected areas and escalating the conflict's impact on regional maritime operations.

events.title: Anchor Handler Runs Aground Off Brazil
events.location: 80 nautical miles northeast of Rio de Janeiro
events.description: An anchor-handler vessel on charter to Petrobras has run aground approximately 80 nautical miles northeast of Rio de Janeiro, potentially impacting local maritime operations and coastal traffic.

events.title: Iranian Tankers Blocked in Gulf of Oman
events.description: The U.S. Navy continues to intercept Iranian tankers in the Gulf of Oman, preventing them from loading cargo, though some vessels are still attempting to run the blockade.

events.title: Russian Drones Strike Commercial Ships Near Odesa
events.description: Russian drones struck three commercial ships, including a Chinese bulker, near Odesa on Monday. The attack caused minor damage and highlights ongoing conflict-related disruptions to maritime freight flows in the Black Sea.

events.title: Captain Arrested Over Tanker Registry Fraud
events.description: Sweden’s prosecutors have opened an investigation into the detained product tanker Sea Owl I, with the Coast Guard reporting the arrest of a new captain linked to the vessel's fake registry.

events.title: Strikes Disrupt Eimskip Shipping Services
events.description: Bosuns and seamen have called a strike against Eimskip, a shipping company operating between Iceland, the Faroe Islands, and Europe, potentially disrupting freight flows in the North Atlantic.

events.title: Dali civil trial delay petition
events.description: Grace Ocean and Synergy Marine have petitioned the court to delay the civil trial regarding the Dali incident, which caused significant damage to Baltimore’s infrastructure.

events.title: CMA CGM Boxship Hit by Iranian Missile in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The CMA CGM San Antonio was struck by an Iranian missile in the Strait of Hormuz, injuring eight crew members. Two of the injured seafarers have since returned home.

events.title: Iran launches Persian Gulf Strait Authority
events.description: Iran has formally launched its self-proclaimed 'Persian Gulf Strait Authority' to administer transit authorization through the Strait of Hormuz, potentially impacting international shipping and regional stability.

events.title: Israeli forces intercept activist flotilla near Crete
events.description: Israeli naval forces intercepted an activist flotilla of small boats heading toward Gaza, creating a potential escalation in the region that could impact maritime routes and freight flows.

events.title: U.S. Waives Sanctions on Russian Oil for Third Time
events.description: The U.S. Treasury has extended a sanctions waiver for the sale of Russian oil already at sea, marking the third such waiver. This policy move affects global oil trade flows and has implications for sanctioned Russian energy exports.

events.title: CMA CGM and Hapag-Lloyd suspend Cuba bookings due to U.S. sanctions
events.description: CMA CGM and Hapag-Lloyd have paused all cargo bookings in and out of Cuba due to the risk of U.S. sanctions and potential asset seizures.

events.title: Ukrainian Grain Ship Blocked at Turkish Port
events.description: Turkey has refused entry to a Greek bulker carrying allegedly stolen Ukrainian grain, following diplomatic pressure from Kyiv. The vessel is now loitering at sea, highlighting tensions over grain ownership and trade routes.

events.title: Adani settles U.S. sanctions violation over Iranian LPG imports
events.description: Adani Enterprises Limited has agreed to pay $275 million to settle U.S. Treasury allegations of sanctions violations related to liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) imports from Iran. The settlement highlights regulatory risks affecting global trade flows involving sanctioned countries.

events.title: U.S. Navy Seizes Iranian Shadow Fleet Tanker
events.description: The U.S. Navy reportedly seized an Iran-linked oil tanker in the Indian Ocean, raising concerns over regional tensions and potential disruptions to oil shipments.

events.title: Container Feeders Collide in East Kalimantan
events.description: Two container feeders collided on a river in East Kalimantan, causing significant above-waterline damage and multiple container spills.

events.title: Med-Mooring Dispute Disrupts Cargo to U.S. Virgin Islands
events.description: A dispute over med-mooring practices has led to the U.S. Coast Guard banning two vessels, disrupting cargo deliveries to the U.S. Virgin Islands. The issue highlights ongoing challenges in maritime logistics for the region.

events.title: Iran controls Hormuz strait tanker traffic
events.description: Iran is reportedly controlling the flow of tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, using the chokepoint for political and propaganda purposes amid rising tensions.

events.title: U.S. Forces Board Iranian Product Tanker
events.description: U.S. forces boarded and redirected an Iranian-flagged product tanker on May 20, raising concerns about regional tensions and potential disruptions to maritime trade in the Gulf.

events.title: South Korea tests Arctic shipping route via Northern Sea Route
events.description: South Korea’s Ministry of Oceans and Fisheries is planning a trial containership voyage along Russia’s Northern Sea Route later this year, tentatively selecting a small container line for the test. The move reflects growing interest in alternative Arctic shipping routes.

events.title: Indonesia imposes export controls on commodities
events.description: Indonesia is centralizing control over the export of commodity products, including palm oil and coal, to combat tax evasion. The move could disrupt global supply chains and affect freight flows.

events.title: UK Eases Restrictions on Russia-Linked Fuel Imports
events.description: The UK has softened new restrictions on fuel imports linked to Russia amid concerns over fuel supply shortages, particularly due to the Strait of Hormuz shutdown impacting jet fuel availability.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz remains closed amid Middle East tensions
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed due to ongoing Middle East tensions, including a reported planned attack on Iran that was delayed. This closure is affecting container freight flows as the Asia-Europe peak season begins.

events.title: NorthPort structural upgrades progress
events.description: NorthPort has completed key testing and installation milestones in its three-year structural upgrade programme, with ongoing work at Pier 16 and Terminal 1 progressing on schedule.

events.title: CEVA opens automated distribution center in China
events.description: CEVA Logistics has opened a new automated distribution center in Alashankou, China, to support freight flows between China, Central Asia, the Caucasus, and Europe. The facility is located in the Alashankou Free Trade Zone and serves as a consolidation hub for LTL cargo.

events.title: West Coast ports see softened cargo volumes
events.description: Import volumes are softening at West Coast ports as Houston and East Coast ports gain cargo share. Flexport reports uneven import demand across North America.

events.title: BNSF raises grain train charges by 472%
events.description: Union Pacific alleges BNSF Railway canceled reciprocal switching rates, causing a 472% increase in grain shipping costs, according to a federal complaint.

events.title: Maersk vessels transit Suez Canal-Red Sea route
events.description: A Maersk vessel has joined a growing number of brand-name ships using the Suez Canal-Red Sea route, indicating a shift in shipping patterns amid ongoing regional disruptions.

events.title: 25% Tariff on Imported Truck Parts Takes Effect
events.description: A 25% tariff on imported medium and heavy-duty truck parts, including engines, transmissions, and chassis components, went into effect on November 1, 2025, following a proclamation by President Trump under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act.

events.title: U.S. Tariff Stacking Impact on Supply Chains
events.description: Tariff stacking is significantly altering supply chain execution in 2025, according to Infios' analysis of U.S. customs data, prompting shifts in trade strategies and logistics planning.

events.title: LA-Long Beach ports report steady container dwell in April
events.description: Southern California ports reported steady container dwell times in April, with truck dwell remaining stable and rail activity showing improvement.

events.title: South Korea tests Arctic container shipping route
events.description: South Korea is supporting a pilot container shipping voyage via the Northern Sea Route, aiming to explore Arctic shipping potential despite challenges like draught limits and expected profitability only after 2040.

events.title: Air cargo enters higher-cost era post-Middle East conflict
events.description: The air freight market is transitioning from acute crisis to a higher-cost environment as jet fuel fears recede, but rates remain elevated and trade routes continue to shift following the Middle East conflict escalation in late February.

events.title: US seizes Iran-linked VLCC in Indian Ocean
events.description: The United States has seized a VLCC carrying Iranian crude in the Indian Ocean, the third Iranian-linked vessel seized since military action against Iran began in February. The vessel, Skywave, was loaded at Iran's Kharg Island and was sailing west of Malaysia.

events.title: Iran seeks to control Strait of Hormuz and impose toll
events.description: Iran is attempting to assert control over the Strait of Hormuz and charge a toll, which has been closed for over 80 days. The move is opposed by the US and could significantly disrupt global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: US indicts container makers for price-fixing
events.description: The US Department of Justice has indicted four major shipping container manufacturers and seven executives for price-fixing, which significantly increased container prices during the pandemic.

events.title: Philippines port expansion with $300m loan
events.description: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) is providing a $300m loan to expand and upgrade container terminals in the Philippines, including the Manila International Container Terminal. The project aims to enhance port capacity and efficiency through technology-enabled infrastructure improvements.

events.title: HMS Dragon heads to Hormuz for security mission
events.description: The Royal Navy destroyer HMS Dragon has transited the Suez Canal and is heading to the Middle East in preparation for a potential multinational mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: South Korean Supertanker Attempts Hormuz Strait Transit
events.description: A South Korean-flagged supertanker is attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz, which could mark a significant shift in shipping routes and regional dynamics if successful.

events.title: South Korea's first Arctic container trial via NSR
events.description: South Korea has selected Panstar Line to operate its first container trial voyage along Russia’s Northern Sea Route, aiming to develop alternative trade corridors between Asia and Europe.

events.title: UAE Pipeline Bypassing Hormuz Reaches 50% Completion
events.description: A new crude oil pipeline being built by the UAE to bypass the Strait of Hormuz is now 50% complete, according to ADNOC CEO Sultan Al Jaber. The project aims to diversify oil export routes and reduce vulnerability to disruptions at the strategic chokepoint.

events.title: UK allows diesel/jet fuel from Russian crude
events.description: The UK government has issued a new general trade licence allowing imports of diesel and jet fuel refined from Russian crude oil, effectively loosening part of its sanctions regime against Russia.

events.title: Shipping issues new Hormuz transit guidance
events.description: The global shipping industry has issued new transit guidance for the Strait of Hormuz due to rising risks, even as the waterway remains technically open. The guidance reflects heightened concerns over safety and operational disruptions in this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Risk
events.description: A prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could cause a severe global energy supply shock, with oil prices potentially reaching $200 per barrel and triggering a global recession, according to Wood Mackenzie.

events.title: U.S. Marines board Iranian oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. Marines boarded and redirected an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman, escalating tensions and potentially disrupting oil shipments in a key maritime region.

events.title: Iran Increases Control Over Hormuz Strait Transit
events.description: Iran is expanding its control over the Strait of Hormuz by implementing checkpoints, diplomatic deals, and passage fees, affecting tanker navigation and raising concerns over potential disruptions to global oil shipments.

events.title: Iran declares new maritime oversight zone in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority has defined a new maritime zone in the Strait of Hormuz, asserting oversight that could impact shipping and regional stability.

events.title: Second wave of US sanctions: 19 vessels designated for deceptive shipping
events.description: US designated 19 more vessels in second wave of Operation Economic Fury, targeting ships using deceptive shipping practices to obfuscate Iran port calls. SAR imagery showed 9 vessels manipulating positions at Basrah Oil Terminal. Builds on first wave (9 vessels, 15 April).

events.title: CMA CGM San Antonio attacked in Hormuz
events.description: French container line CMA CGM confirmed its vessel San Antonio was attacked while transiting the Strait of Hormuz. One of several merchant-vessel attacks during the wider crisis; 17 ships damaged, 7 abandoned across the crisis.

events.title: US East Coast ports — dockworker strike risk
events.description: Tens of thousands of ILA dockworkers threatening strike if union cannot reach a deal to renew its contract with terminal operators. Could shut down major US East Coast ports as early as next week.

events.title: DHL CEO warns Gulf shock approaches "tipping point"
events.description: DHL Group CEO Tobias Meyer warned that sustained disruption in Gulf crude flows could push the global economy toward a tipping point. Routes tightening, freight markets constrained, shipping rates rising — especially on Asia-Europe lanes.

events.title: Farmer-trucker blockades disrupt Mexican ports & highways
events.description: Major farmer-trucker blockades across Mexico are disrupting highways, slowing port and border operations, and forcing logistics providers to reroute freight. Affecting both export and import flows through Mexican ports.

events.title: UPS Surge Emergency Fee in effect
events.description: UPS implemented a Surge Emergency Fee. Most international shipments incur a $0.23/lb charge; China and Hong Kong → US shipments face $0.32/lb. Applies to premium services (Worldwide Express, Express Freight).

events.title: Port of Manzanillo destabilised by CJNG cartel violence
events.description: Death of CJNG cartel leader "El Mencho" triggered violent retaliation across western Mexico — disrupting highways, rattling the Port of Manzanillo (Mexico's busiest Pacific port), and threatening US-Mexico cross-border trade flows.

events.title: Russian LNG carriers rerouting via Cape of Good Hope
events.description: Five Russia-flagged LNG carriers tracked sailing from Arctic ports to South China Sea via Cape of Good Hope and Sunda Strait throughout April–May, following the Arctic Metagaz drone strike. Tactic spreading beyond the LNG segment.

events.title: US imposes 10% global tariff after Supreme Court ruling
events.description: Trump unveiled a new 10% global tariff after the Supreme Court ruled many of his earlier country-specific tariffs unconstitutional. Companies advised to track entries, file protests, and prepare for billions-of-dollars refund process.

events.title: Jawaharlal Nehru Port: transhipment volume up 700%
events.description: India's Jawaharlal Nehru Port has rapidly transformed into a major transhipment hub, with volumes up more than 700% from February baselines. Saudi Arabia and Singapore are also emerging as Gulf-diversion destinations.

events.title: Russian LNG carrier Arctic Metagaz hit by drone strike
events.description: The laden LNG carrier Arctic Metagaz was struck by a drone east of Malta. Crew abandoned ship; vessel later anchored off Benghazi after EU-sanctioned salvage. Triggered Russia LNG carriers avoiding Mediterranean transits.

events.title: Red Sea attacks resume — Bab el-Mandeb transits halved
events.description: Houthis resumed attacks on Red Sea shipping after the October 2025 ceasefire collapsed. Bab el-Mandeb transits stuck at ~50% pre-attack levels. Carriers default to Cape of Good Hope routing — adding 10-14 days Asia <-> Europe.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis — 1,550+ vessels stranded
events.description: Iran has largely blocked Strait of Hormuz shipping since US-Israel struck Iranian targets on 28 Feb. As of early May: 22,500 mariners trapped on 1,550+ vessels; traffic ~5-15% of pre-war volume. US naval blockade since 13 April. Iran allowing limited transit via northern coastal corridor under IRGC monitoring.

events.title: Port of Tema operating with crane outages
events.description: Tema port experiencing significant crane outages. Berthing delays for window vessels extend up to 36 hours; non-window vessels subject to berth availability. Operating under strict pro-forma move limits.

events.title: Port of Mombasa: 5+ day waits, equipment shortages
events.description: Mombasa terminal experiencing prolonged congestion and reduced productivity due to equipment shortages and high transhipment volumes diverted from Gulf. 7-day average vessel wait time around 5+ days.

events.title: Container terminal fire at Port of Karachi
events.description: Fire in the container terminals disrupted operations on 16 January. Over 20 containers were impacted; the fire forced suspension of handling operations for two vessels.

events.title: Panama Canal — drought-induced transit limits ongoing
events.description: Dwindling water levels in Panama Canal continue to constrain global supply chains. Compounds the Suez & Hormuz disruptions — with East Coast US <-> Asia traffic looking for any working alternative.
